spec.title: Engaging Motion Graphics for Schugle
spec.grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the Freelancer.com brief in db_all.json): Schugle is a German AI web product for teachers that helps create/formulate/structure/revise Foerderplaene (individual support plans) while protecting sensitive pupil data via anonymised placeholders (real pupil names never go to the AI). The client wants THREE related promotional/explanatory videos with one coherent look: (1) a 10-second clip also usable as a SILENT website hero/background loop that works without voiceover, (2) a 30-second promotional/social/reel version, (3) a 2-minute explainer that may reuse material from the 10s and 30s. German on-screen text is REQUIRED; the client may supply/change German voiceover later, so German text must stay EDITABLE (text layers, not flattened). The narrative direction is given verbatim: "Messy notes -> structured thoughts -> protected placeholders -> AI-assisted formulation -> clear Foerderplan," plus the Schugle tilted "g" icon as a recurring motif and tone that is calm/credible/professional/modern — explicitly NOT generic education stock (no happy teachers, smiling children, chalkboards, graduation caps, cartoon kids). The client also asks for: clean no-text versions, separate audio tracks for music/SFX/VO placeholders, subtitle SRT/VTT if subtitles are used, and a full list of fonts/stock/icons/templates/music WITH license terms. Budget up to $1,500 USD; tools the client expects include After Effects/Premiere Pro/Canva/Figma. The reference tone sites foerder-fokus.de and schugle.de/einstieg are named in the brief.
ENRICHED (added only where the brief implies it, to make it a runnable connector benchmark, NOT to change the deliverable): (a) The exact narrative-beat extraction is operationalised by running media_summarize over a supplied reference tutorial clip (the brief explicitly points to existing schugle.de/einstieg tutorial videos as reference) so the storyboard is GROUNDED in the real product flow rather than invented. (b) The Express template-track (search_design -> fill_text -> animate_design) is chosen as the in-connector motion path because the capability sheet routes motion-graphics output through it; the brief's "templates, AI tools, stock assets, custom animation, or a mix" line explicitly permits templates. (c) Specific aspect ratios 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1 are enriched from the brief's stated channels (website hero = 16:9, LinkedIn/social/reel = 9:16 and 1:1) — the brief names the channels, the exact ratios are the standard mapping. (d) A licensed Stock music bed is enriched from the brief's explicit requirement to deliver "separate audio files/tracks for music" WITH license terms. (e) The exact German on-screen copy strings and the Foerderplan reference data row are enriched to concrete sample text, faithful to the brief's described content (Foerderplaene, anonymisation, less paperwork). No deliverable is invented beyond the three videos + editable text + clean versions + audio + license list the client asked for.
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a coherent German motion-graphics campaign for the ed-tech product Schugle — a 10s silent website-hero loop, a 30s promo/reel, and a 2-minute explainer — built from a branded Express motion template with editable German on-screen text, reframed to 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1, scored with a licensed Stock music bed, and accompanied by clean no-text versions, an SRT caption file, and a font/stock/template/music license manifest.
spec.full_brief:
Schugle (pronounced "shoe" + the "gle" from "Google") is a German web product that helps teachers create, formulate, structure and revise Foerderplaene (individual pupil support plans) with AI — while protecting sensitive pupil data by replacing real names with anonymised placeholders before anything reaches the AI. The current website is factual and dry; the client wants a coherent visual campaign that makes the idea attractive, memorable and emotionally clear while staying calm, credible, professional and suitable for schools. The mandated narrative arc is "Messy notes -> structured thoughts -> protected placeholders -> AI-assisted formulation -> clear Foerderplan," with the tilted Schugle "g" icon as a recurring motif. The client explicitly forbids generic education clichés (happy teachers, smiling children, chalkboards, graduation caps, cartoon kids, generic "school app" imagery).

Produce THREE related videos that read as one campaign: (1) a 10-second clip that also works as a SILENT website hero/background loop (must read with no voiceover and be visually calm), (2) a 30-second promotional/social/reel version, and (3) a 2-minute explainer that reuses material from the first two. German on-screen text is required and MUST remain editable — the client may swap German copy or add German voiceover later, so text must live as editable layers, never flattened into the only final file. Required German on-screen strings (faithful to the product): "Verstreute Notizen" (scattered notes), "Struktur statt Stapel" (structure instead of piles), "Echte Namen bleiben geschützt" (real names stay protected) with an anonymised placeholder like "[Schüler:in A]", "KI-gestützte Formulierung" (AI-assisted formulation), and "Klarer Förderplan. Weniger Schreibarbeit." (clear support plan, less paperwork).

Constraints and deliverables: master the campaign at 1080p, 30fps, H.264 MP4. Deliver each of the three videos in three aspect ratios — 16:9 (1920×1080, website hero), 9:16 (1080×1920, reel/Stories) and 1:1 (1080×1080, feed) — preserving the legible position of the German text in every reframe. Provide a clean NO-TEXT version of the 10s hero loop (silent, for the website background). Score the 30s and 2-min with a single licensed Stock music bed (calm, modern, non-corporate-cheesy) at -16 LUFS, delivered ALSO as a separate WAV track; the 10s hero stays silent. If captions are shown, supply a German SRT. Finally deliver a license manifest (CSV/MD) listing every font, Stock asset, template and music track used, with license terms and the Adobe Stock license IDs. The visual style must echo the calm, trustworthy German-school tone of the named reference (foerder-fokus.de) without copying it, and must be grounded in how the real product actually flows (see the supplied reference tutorial clip).

inputs.name: schugle_g_icon.svg
inputs.gen_prompt: A minimal, modern logo mark: a single lowercase letter 'g' tilted ~12 degrees to the right, geometric sans-serif, rounded terminals, solid deep-teal (#0E7C86) on transparent background. Clean vector-ready line art, no gradient, no drop shadow, no extra text, centered with even margin. Flat 2D, crisp edges suitable for tracing to SVG. Single colour, high contrast.
inputs.realism_notes: Brand mark, not a photo — must be flat, single-colour, perfectly clean so image_vectorize produces a tidy SVG. No photoreal texture. Tilted 'g' is the brief's named visual motif.

inputs.name: schugle_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A horizontal logotype reading 'Schugle' in a calm modern geometric sans-serif, lowercase except the capital S, deep-teal (#0E7C86) on transparent background, the lowercase 'g' tilted ~12 degrees to echo the icon. Tight even kerning, no tagline, no shadow, flat single colour, high resolution, sharp anti-aliased edges. Centered with generous transparent margin.
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing brand asset — gemini-3-pro-image (Nano-Banana-Pro) handles crisp legible logotype better than a photoreal model. Transparent PNG so it composites cleanly over template scenes.

inputs.name: schugle_reference_tutorial.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A 45-second calm screen-recording-style explainer of a German ed-tech web app for teachers, 1080p, native German voiceover (clear neutral female voice, unhurried). Shows: a teacher's cluttered handwritten notes on a desk, then a clean web interface where scattered notes are organised into a structured support-plan (Förderplan) form, a step where a real pupil name field is replaced by a protected placeholder badge reading '[Schüler:in A]', then an AI-assist panel suggesting a polished German sentence, ending on a tidy finished plan. Muted professional palette (teal, warm grey, off-white), soft even lighting, real UI typography. Documentary product-demo feel, NOT flashy sci-fi, no cartoon elements, no stock-footage teachers smiling at camera.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the brief's referenced 'existing basic tutorial' the agent must SUMMARISE to lift narrative beats. Use veo-3.1-generate-preview at 1080p with native audio (NOT veo-fast/720p) so media_summarize has real speech + scenes to transcribe. German VO is essential because the beats must map to the German product flow.

inputs.name: foerderplan_sample_data.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a small JSON object of German on-screen copy for a Schugle motion-graphics campaign, with keys: scene1_messy='Verstreute Notizen', scene2_structure='Struktur statt Stapel', scene3_privacy='Echte Namen bleiben geschützt', placeholder='[Schüler:in A]', scene4_ai='KI-gestützte Formulierung', scene5_payoff='Klarer Förderplan. Weniger Schreibarbeit.', cta='Schugle — Förderpläne, klar und geschützt.' Also include an array 'srt_lines' of 6 short German caption strings matching these scenes. Valid UTF-8 JSON, correct German umlauts and the colon-form 'Schüler:in'.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the fill_text step and the SRT. gemini-2.5-flash for clean structured German data. Umlauts and the gender-colon form must be correct — a real German ed product would use 'Schüler:in'.

inputs.name: brand_palette_and_type.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a JSON brand sheet for Schugle: primary='#0E7C86' (deep teal), secondary='#F2EFE9' (warm off-white), ink='#23323A' (slate), accent='#E8A23D' (muted amber, used sparingly), font_primary preference='geometric humanist sans (e.g. Source Sans 3 / Inter)', tone keywords=['calm','credible','professional','modern','trustworthy'], forbidden=['chalkboard','graduation cap','cartoon child','smiling teacher stock']. Valid JSON.
inputs.realism_notes: Feeds font_recommend and the template-search query so the chosen Express template and font stay on-brand. Encodes the brief's explicit do-not-use list so the agent self-checks.

outputs.name: schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, 30fps, H.264 MP4, exactly 10.0s, SILENT (no audio track), seamless loop, NO on-screen text (clean hero-background version)
outputs.rubric_hook: Loops seamlessly; truly silent; no text baked in; calm enough for a website hero; tilted-g motif present; reads as same campaign as the others.

outputs.name: schugle_hero_10s_16x9_text.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, 30fps, H.264 MP4, 10.0s, silent, WITH German on-screen text ('Verstreute Notizen' -> payoff), text from editable layer
outputs.rubric_hook: German strings correct incl. umlauts; text legible and well-placed; identical motion to clean version minus text; still silent.

outputs.name: schugle_promo_30s_set.mp4
outputs.spec: Three files: 30s at 16:9 (1920×1080), 9:16 (1080×1920), 1:1 (1080×1080); 30fps H.264; music bed at -16 LUFS; German on-screen text preserved & legible in every ratio
outputs.rubric_hook: Subject/text stay in-frame and legible after each reframe; music synced & loudness-correct; narrative beats match the summarized reference; consistent look across ratios.

outputs.name: schugle_explainer_2min_set.mp4
outputs.spec: Three files: 120s at 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1; 30fps H.264; music bed -16 LUFS; visibly reuses 10s + 30s material; full 5-beat narrative
outputs.rubric_hook: Demonstrably reuses earlier assets (continuity); all five German beats present and ordered per the storyboard; pacing reads as a 2-min explainer not a stretched promo.

outputs.name: schugle_music_bed.wav
outputs.spec: Stereo WAV, 48kHz, ~120s, calm modern non-corporate; the licensed Stock track delivered as a separate stem; loudness -16 LUFS integrated
outputs.rubric_hook: Delivered separately (editable), correct format/loudness, matches the licensed Stock asset id in the manifest, tone fits 'calm/credible'.

outputs.name: schugle_captions_de.srt
outputs.spec: Valid SRT, German, ~6 cues timed to the 30s promo, UTF-8 with correct umlauts and 'Schüler:in' colon-form
outputs.rubric_hook: Valid SRT timecodes; German text matches on-screen copy; umlauts/colon-form correct; cue timing lands on the right scenes.

outputs.name: schugle_license_manifest.csv
outputs.spec: CSV/MD listing every font, Adobe Stock asset (with license id), Express template id, and music track used, each with license terms and source
outputs.rubric_hook: Every consumed asset accounted for; Stock license id present and matches the licensed download; fonts + template id listed; rights line for Schugle reuse included.

connector_workflow.output: schugle_g_icon_clean.svg (tidy vector trace of the tilted-g motif)
connector_workflow.note: Trace the supplied tilted-g mark into a clean SVG so the motif can be scaled losslessly across every scene and ratio. First node of the brand-identity chain.

connector_workflow.output: font_choice (a calm geometric-humanist sans matching the wordmark + brand sheet)
connector_workflow.note: Lock a brand-appropriate typeface for the German on-screen text, constrained by brand_palette_and_type.json tone keywords and the wordmark look. Feeds the template/fill steps.

connector_workflow.output: reference_beats.json (transcript + scene summary of the real product flow)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Summarise the supplied German tutorial clip to lift the genuine narrative beats (notes -> structure -> placeholder -> AI -> finished plan) so the storyboard is grounded in the actual product, not invented.

connector_workflow.output: express_motion_template_id (a calm modern explainer/title-card motion-graphics Express template)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Search the Express template library for a calm, modern motion template whose scene count matches the 5 summarized beats; query is shaped by reference_beats.json and the brand palette (and the brief's do-not-use list).

connector_workflow.output: filled_master_design (template with the 5 German strings + wordmark/icon placed, text kept as editable layers)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Fill the template's placeholder text with the German strings from foerderplan_sample_data.json (umlauts + 'Schüler:in' intact) and drop in the wordmark + traced g-icon. Text-only edit preserves layout and keeps strings editable per the brief.

connector_workflow.output: music_bed_licensed (calm modern Stock music track + license id)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. License a calm, non-corporate Stock music bed sized to the 2-min explainer, guided by the reference tone. Capture the license id for the manifest. The hero loop stays silent; this scores the 30s + 2min.

connector_workflow.output: master_motion_10s (animated 16:9 master of the filled design, the 10s hero base)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Animate the filled Express design into the 10s motion master (fade/slide/zoom on the 5 beats, subtle tilted-g motif animation). This is the seed asset every later video is derived from — core output->input pivot.

connector_workflow.output: master_motion_30s and master_motion_120s (longer-duration animated cuts reusing the 10s scenes)
connector_workflow.note: Produce the 30s and 2-min animated masters by extending the same filled design with the additional summarized beats, deliberately reusing the 10s scene blocks so all three read as one campaign (brief: 2-min may reuse 10s/30s material).

connector_workflow.output: explainer_assembly_120s (assembled 2-min explainer reusing 10s + 30s segments in beat order)
connector_workflow.note: Assemble the 2-minute explainer by sequencing the reused 10s and 30s motion segments into the full 5-beat arc — concrete reuse of earlier outputs to build the longest deliverable.

connector_workflow.output: reframed_set (each of 10s/30s/120s masters reframed to 9:16 and 1:1, subject- & text-aware)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Reframe every master to 9:16 (1080×1920) and 1:1 (1080×1080) keeping the German text legible and in-frame; 16:9 masters already exist. Yields the full 3-video × 3-ratio matrix.

Clean and normalise the licensed music bed toward -16 LUFS and export it as the separate WAV stem the brief requires (separate audio track for music). Note: no VO is baked — the brief says German VO may be added later.

connector_workflow.output: cc_delivery_folder_structure (Hero/, Promo/, Explainer/, Audio/, Editable/, Captions/, License/)
connector_workflow.note: Create the CC-storage delivery folder tree so the multi-ratio video matrix, clean no-text hero, audio stem, SRT and manifest land in an organised, client-ready handoff.

connector_workflow.output: uploaded_deliverables (all MP4s, the clean no-text hero, music WAV, German SRT, and license CSV in their CC folders → URNs)
connector_workflow.note: Upload every final deliverable into the CC folder tree from step 12: 3 videos × 3 ratios, clean no-text 10s hero, music_bed_clean.wav, schugle_captions_de.srt (built from foerderplan_sample_data.json srt_lines), and the license manifest CSV (with the Stock license id from step 6 and the template id from step 4).

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely sequential, not parallel. (1) The supplied reference tutorial is summarised (step 3) and that beat list is what shapes the template search (step 4), the storyboard, the Stock-music sizing (step 6), and the extended cuts (step 8) — a content output feeding three downstream decisions. (2) The brand-identity outputs feed forward: the traced g-icon (step 1) and recommended font (step 2) both flow into fill_text (step 5) and animate_design (step 7). (3) The core pivot is the Express design pipeline: search_design (4) -> fill_text (5, consuming the German data + icon + font) -> animate_design (7) produces the 10s master, which is then the literal input to the 30s/2min extension (8), the quick-cut explainer assembly (9), and the multi-ratio reframe (10). Each video file is derived from the one before, not regenerated — the 2-min explainer (9) is built by re-sequencing the 10s (7) and 30s (8) segments, satisfying the brief's 'may reuse material.' (4) The licensed music (6) is cleaned into a separate stem (11). (5) Finally the folder tree (12) is created and every produced artifact — 9 video files, the clean no-text hero, the WAV stem, the SRT derived from the same German data used in step 5, and the license manifest carrying the step-6 Stock id and step-4 template id — is uploaded together (13). Inputs are heavily reused: the g-icon spans steps 1/2/5/7, the German copy data spans 4/5/11/13, the reference summary spans 3/4/6/8/9.
spec.difficulty_rationale: HARD on every axis a long-horizon agent struggles with. (a) Long horizon: 13 dependent connector calls across three tool groups with no shortcut — skipping the summarize step de-grounds the storyboard, skipping the icon trace breaks brand continuity. (b) Heavy state tracking: the agent must carry the 5-beat order, the exact German strings (umlauts + 'Schüler:in' colon-form), the chosen font, the template id, and the Stock license id all the way to the final manifest — and must keep the clean no-text hero and the text version as two distinct branches of the same 10s master. (c) Exact-spec pressure: nine video files at three precise resolutions (1920×1080 / 1080×1920 / 1080×1080), 30fps, 10.0/30/120s exact durations, a SILENT loop vs scored versions, music at -16 LUFS — any wrong dimension, a baked-in caption on the 'clean' version, or audio on the silent hero fails a rubric hook. (d) Massive asset reuse and continuity: three videos must read as one campaign, so the agent must reuse the 10s/30s segments to build the 2-min rather than regenerate, and keep German text legible after subject-aware reframing into portrait and square — a classic place where text drifts out of frame. (e) Faithfulness traps: the brief explicitly forbids generic-school clichés and demands editable text + a complete license manifest, so a plausible-looking but flattened or unlicensed output silently fails. The agent must also correctly NOT add voiceover (the client supplies German VO later) — over-helping breaks the spec.

issues: CAPABILITY MISUSE (step 11): media_enhance_speech is applied to a licensed instrumental Stock MUSIC bed. The capability sheet (line 69) defines media_enhance_speech as 'clean/denoise/enhance a VOICE recording (podcast/VO cleanup)' — it is a speech tool, not a music processor and not a loudness normalizer. There is NO connector tool that normalizes audio to a target -16 LUFS integrated, so the brief's '-16 LUFS WAV stem' requirement (schugle_music_bed.wav rubric hook) has no real tool behind it. Running speech-denoise on a music-only track is wrong-modality and may degrade it.

MISSING GENERATING STEP for the clean no-text hero (output schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4). fill_text (step 5) produces ONE text-bearing filled design; animate_design (step 7) animates THAT text design. Only one animate_design path to the 10s master exists, so the 'NO on-screen text' clean version is asserted as an output but is never actually produced by any branch. The text and no-text versions are described as 'two distinct branches of the same 10s master' in the difficulty_rationale, but the workflow contains only the text branch.

OVER-CLAIMED DURATION CONTROL: the sheet describes animate_design only as 'add motion to an Express design (fade/slide/zoom)' with no documented duration/fps/codec parameters. The spec demands exact 10.0s / 30s / 120s durations, 30fps, H.264. Step 8 further claims a SINGLE animate_design call emits BOTH master_motion_30s AND master_motion_120s (two different-length files from one call) — unsupported by the sheet.

TEXT-LEGIBILITY AFTER REFRAME NOT GUARANTEED: video_resize is 'subject-aware' and 'same len' (line 68), i.e. it reframes around the SUBJECT, not around baked-in text. Multiple rubric hooks require German on-screen text to 'stay in-frame and legible in every ratio' after reframe to 9:16/1:1. Subject-aware autoreframe routinely crops lower-third/title text out of frame; the tool cannot guarantee this rubric hook.

SRT and license CSV deliverables (schugle_captions_de.srt, schugle_license_manifest.csv) have NO generating connector step. Step 13 only UPLOADS them. No tool in the chain authors the SRT timecodes or writes the CSV — they must be authored outside the connector, but the spec lists them as graded deliverables without a generating action, leaving an io-completeness gap.

video_create_quick_cut (step 9) is 'NOT speech-aware' and produces an AI highlight/sizzle reel from clips (line 66-67). Using it to deterministically ASSEMBLE a 2-min explainer 'in beat order' to an exact 120.0s is a stretch: quick-cut selects engaging segments by visual-engagement metrics, it is not a timeline sequencer and does not guarantee exact duration or beat order.
fixes: Replace step 11 (media_enhance_speech on the music bed) with an honest path: either deliver the licensed Stock music track AS-IS as the separate WAV stem (drop the -16 LUFS / denoise claim, since no connector tool normalizes loudness), or move loudness-normalization out of the connector chain and label it an out-of-band/manual step. Remove the rubric hook that grades '-16 LUFS integrated' unless a real loudness tool exists. media_enhance_speech should only ever touch a VO/voice asset.

Add an explicit no-text branch for the clean hero: e.g. a second search_design+animate_design (or animate_design on the un-filled template / a text-layer-hidden variant) to produce schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4. As written, that output has no generator. If the connector cannot hide/remove the text layer, drop the clean-no-text deliverable or generate it from a separate un-filled design.

Soften or remove the exact-duration / fps / codec guarantees attached to animate_design, or add a note that these are best-effort outputs not parameterized by the tool. Split step 8 into two separate animate_design calls (one per duration) instead of claiming one call yields both 30s and 120s, and verify animate_design accepts a duration parameter before grading exact 10.0/30/120s.

Either drop the 'text legible & in-frame after reframe' rubric hooks for video_resize, or change the approach so German text is added AFTER reframing (per-ratio text placement) rather than baked into the master before subject-aware resize. Note explicitly that video_resize is subject-aware, not text-aware.

Add explicit authoring steps (or clearly mark as out-of-connector) for the SRT and the license CSV: the SRT is built from foerderplan_sample_data.json srt_lines and the CSV is assembled from the step-4 template id + step-6 Stock license id. State that these are authored locally then uploaded, so the io contract is complete.

For step 9, do not rely on video_create_quick_cut for deterministic exact-length beat-ordered assembly. Either acknowledge it produces a best-effort highlight cut (and relax the 'exactly 120s, full 5-beat ordered' rubric), or move the 2-min assembly to a deterministic sequencing path outside this connector set, since no listed tool is a timeline editor.
